assertions.title: Large Repo Performance

steps.action: Create combined test repo from fastify (74,597 LOC) + ripgrep (52,266 LOC) at /tmp/vera-test-100k
Initialize git repo for vera compatibility
Run `time vera index .` on combined repo
steps.observed: Indexing completed in 96.54s (1m36.564s wall-clock). Files parsed: 591, Chunks: 8274, Embeddings: 8274. Binary skipped: 10.

evidence.consoleErrors: 3 embedding API rate limit warnings (6-7s retry delays each), 1 UTF-8 parse error on sherlock.br

assertions.issues: Indexing took 96.54s, exceeding the 60s target. API rate limiting contributed ~18-21s of delays (3 rate limit retries at 6-7s each). Even without rate limits, estimated time (~75-78s) would still exceed 60s. The bottleneck appears to be embedding API latency for 8274 chunks.

steps.action: Measure source size of combined repo (excluding .git, .vera, node_modules, target)
Query metadata.db for files from excluded directories
Search for git-internal content (HEAD refs pack-refs)
steps.observed: 10 results returned, 0 from .git/ — all from legitimate source files

steps.action: Search for IDE config terms (vscode settings idea workspace)
Verify language breakdown shows no unexpected files
steps.observed: python: 80 files, unknown: 122 files, toml: 5, yaml: 8, markdown: 6, json: 2, bash: 1. Total: 224 files, all from legitimate paths.

assertions.title: Binary Files and Large Files

steps.action: Create temp dir with binary files (image.png, photo.jpg, compiled.o) and normal source (main.py, large_file.py)
steps.expected: Test directory with mixed file types
Binary files skipped, source files indexed
steps.observed: Files parsed: 3, Binary skipped: 3. Metadata confirms 0 chunks for binary files.

steps.action: Create separate test with 3.5MB Python file (exceeds 1MB default threshold)
steps.observed: Files parsed: 2, Skipped 'Too large': 1. The 3.5MB file was excluded.

steps.action: Verify threshold is configurable in code
steps.observed: IndexingConfig.max_file_size_bytes (default 1MB) in config.rs. Unit test confirms configurability. No CLI flag for runtime override.

assertions.issues: Threshold is configurable at the Rust API level but no CLI flag is exposed yet for runtime override. This is a minor gap.

frictions.description: No single benchmark repo in .bench/repos/ exceeds 100K LOC. Had to combine fastify (74K) + ripgrep (52K) into /tmp/vera-test-100k to create a 126K LOC test repo.
frictions.resolution: Created combined repo by copying both repos (excluding .vera/) to a temp directory.

frictions.description: `vera stats` returns 'not yet implemented' — could not use it for language breakdown verification. Had to query metadata.db directly via sqlite3.
frictions.resolution: Used Python sqlite3 module to directly query the .vera/metadata.db for file paths and language breakdown.

frictions.description: Embedding API rate limiting during 100K+ LOC indexing test added ~18-21s of delay, making it harder to assess true indexing performance.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed (VAL-IDX-007, VAL-DISC-001, VAL-DISC-002), 1 failed (VAL-IDX-006: 126K LOC indexed in 96.54s, exceeding 60s target — embedding API latency is the bottleneck, with rate limit retries adding ~18-21s).
